# Release fixture (minimal)

This directory is the **canonical regression anchor** for the release artifact chain (package-release to verify-release).

It is not generated by CI. Build it once from a known-good commit and commit the contents:

```bash
./scripts/build_release_fixture.sh
# Windows: .\scripts\build_release_fixture.ps1
```

Then add and commit `tests/fixtures/release_fixture_minimal/`. Commit at least: `receipts/`, `RISK_REGISTER_BUNDLE.v0.1.json`, `RELEASE_MANIFEST.v0.1.json`, and any files listed in RELEASE_MANIFEST (so hash verification passes). Optionally include MANIFEST.v0.1.json, ENV/, BENCHMARK_CARD.md, metadata.json for a full snapshot.

The test `tests/test_release_fixture_verify_release.py` runs `verify-release --release-dir <fixture> --strict-fingerprints` (via the verify_release API with strict_fingerprints=True). The CI job **release-fixture-verify** runs that test on every PR. Any change that breaks release invariants fails the gate even if the golden suite passes. The fixture must be built when package-release produces evidence bundles that include all provenance fingerprints (coordination_policy_fingerprint, memory_policy_fingerprint, rbac_policy_fingerprint, tool_registry_fingerprint); otherwise the build script fails at step 4 or the test fails.
